question: Will Kim Min-Jae record the most red cards in 2025-26 UEFA Champions League?
description:
This market will resolve to the player who records the most red cards through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

For the purpose of this market, both direct red cards, or red cards that result from the accumulation of two yellow cards in the same match will be considered in the final count.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus tightly clusters around combative midfielders and defenders like Álvaro Fernández Carreras and Kim Min-Jae, reflecting their high foul volumes and yellow card tallies early in the UCL league phase—Fernández with two bookings in Benfica's opener, Min-Jae teetering on Napoli's backline intensity. Mikel Merino and Konrad Laimer follow closely, buoyed by physical styles in Real Sociedad's and Bayern's packed schedules, where midfield battles amplify sending-off risks via double yellows. Martín Zubimendi, Declan Rice, and others trail but gain from remaining fixtures against aggressive rivals, underscoring uncertain disciplinary dynamics as no player yet holds multiple reds, keeping the market volatile amid referee scrutiny and fatigue.

eventMetadata.context_description: Declan Rice, Álvaro Fernández Carreras, Mikel Merino, Martín Zubimendi, and Konrad Laimer lead the UEFA Champions League yellow card race with four bookings apiece after six league-phase matchdays, fueling the tight trader consensus around 40-45% implied probabilities. These combative midfielders and defenders—Rice and the newly arrived Merino anchoring Arsenal's engine room, Fernández as Benfica's aggressive left-back, Zubimendi patrolling Real Sociedad's midfield, Laimer grinding for Bayern—share high-minute workloads and foul-prone styles in dense fixtures. Kim Min-Jae trails closely at three for Bayern, while Lamine Yamal's lower tally reflects his winger role. Remaining schedules, including Arsenal's favorable run and Bayern's clashes, sustain the deadlock absent suspensions.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Olise leads the UEFA Champions League assist race with three, but the market remains tight as Arda Güler, Khvicha Kvaratskhelia, and Leandro Trossard each sit on two, separated by slim margins after just three matchdays in the league phase. Trader consensus reflects this parity, pricing Olise at 31% implied probability amid Bayern Munich's high-scoring run, while Güler's creative bursts for Real Madrid and Kvaratskhelia's Napoli flair keep them close. Factors like Yamal's Barcelona emergence, Rashford's United revival, and Griezmann's set-piece prowess fuel volatility, with eight league games plus knockouts ahead favoring consistent chance-creators on potent attacks over any single standout.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé leads trader consensus at 41.5% implied probability for most Champions League goal contributions, fueled by his six goals across four league-phase matches and Real Madrid's perfect record, positioning him for deep tournament runs. Michael Olise surges to 29.3% on Bayern Munich's attacking dominance, with three goals and four assists in UCL play, including key contributions in their 4-0 rout of Celtic. Fermín López (24.9%) benefits from Barcelona's high-scoring form, netting three goals himself amid Raphinha's exploits, while Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (24.6%) and Julian Álvarez (21.6%) draw support from Napoli and Atlético Madrid's counterattacking threats. Vitinha's 21.0% reflects PSG's midfield creativity, though Harry Kane lags at 14.0% despite six goals, as Bayern spreads contributions. No major injuries alter lineups ahead of matchday five.

This market will resolve to the player who records the most goal contributions combined through all main tournament rounds of the 2025-26 UEFA Champions League competition.

For the purpose of this market, a goal contribution is defined exclusively as the number of combined goals and assists a player has recorded.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by UEFA Champions League rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2025-26 Champions League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no official leader declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé leads trader consensus at 41.5% implied probability for most Champions League goal contributions, fueled by his six goals across four league-phase matches and Real Madrid's perfect record, positioning him for deep tournament runs. Michael Olise surges to 29.3% on Bayern Munich's attacking dominance, with three goals and four assists in UCL play, including key contributions in their 4-0 rout of Celtic. Fermín López (24.9%) benefits from Barcelona's high-scoring form, netting three goals himself amid Raphinha's exploits, while Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (24.6%) and Julian Álvarez (21.6%) draw support from Napoli and Atlético Madrid's counterattacking threats. Vitinha's 21.0% reflects PSG's midfield creativity, though Harry Kane lags at 14.0% despite six goals, as Bayern spreads contributions. No major injuries alter lineups ahead of matchday five.

question: U.S. evacuates Jerusalem embassy by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the U.S. government announces a full evacuation of the U.S. Embassy in Jerusalem, Israel, or a full evacuation of the embassy is otherwise confirmed by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A full evacuation announced within this market's timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether an actual evacuation subsequently takes place within the timeframe.

Announcements of a partial evacuation, where some staff are intended to remain, will not count.

The resolution source will be official statements from the U.S. government, or a consensus of credible reporting confirming the evacuation of the embassy.

events.title: U.S. evacuates Jerusalem embassy by March 31?
events.description:

A full evacuation announced within this market's timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether an actual evacuation subsequently takes place within the timeframe.

Announcements of a partial evacuation, where some staff are intended to remain, will not count.

The resolution source will be official statements from the U.S. government, or a consensus of credible reporting confirming the evacuation of the embassy.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly expects no US evacuation of the Jerusalem embassy by March 31, anchored by the State Department's lack of any evacuation orders or announcements amid routine operations. Regional tensions persist—with Israel-Hamas conflict and Hezbollah border clashes—but travel advisories remain at Level 3 for most areas, short of ordered departures seen in prior crises like Afghanistan. The embassy's post-2018 permanence underscores policy continuity, with no primary signals of threat escalation. High confidence reflects traders' assessment of historical resilience during flare-ups. Realistic catalysts for reversal include abrupt Iranian proxy advances or direct attacks on US facilities, though current intelligence points to contained risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: The high trader consensus against a U.S. evacuation of the Beirut embassy by March 31 stems primarily from the State Department's stance that the facility remains fully operational, with only voluntary departures for non-essential personnel and family members authorized since October 2023 amid Israel-Hezbollah border tensions. No official orders for full evacuation have issued, despite Level 4 travel advisories for Lebanon citing risks from conflict spillover. Recent diplomatic efforts, including U.S.-brokered talks to de-escalate northern Israel clashes, and stable embassy staffing levels reinforce this assessment, as traders weigh the absence of acute triggers like major escalations or direct threats to the compound against historical patterns where evacuations follow explicit security directives.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on low odds for U.S. evacuation of the Baghdad embassy stems from the absence of any State Department ordered departure or drawdown announcements, with the facility operating normally amid reduced militia attacks on American forces in Iraq. Recent Israel-Iran exchanges, including Israel's October 26 strikes following Tehran's missile barrage, have heightened regional tensions but not prompted embassy-specific measures, as confirmed by official updates. Historical resilience during past sieges, like 2020, bolsters skepticism. Key upcoming catalysts include potential Iranian retaliation by late October and the U.S. presidential election on November 5, which could shift Middle East policy and influence probabilities.

question: Will Daniel Ennis win the 2026 Dublin-central by-election?
description:
A by-election for a seat from the Dublin Central constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026to fill the vacancy left by the resignation of Paschal Donohoe.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Dublin Central by-election market strongly backs independent Daniel Ennis at 75.5% implied probability, propelled by local polls showing his double-digit lead over rivals, fueled by voter frustration with established parties on housing and crime issues. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan trails at 13.1%, supported by organizational strength but hampered by national headwinds. Gerry Hutch's 3.6% niche reflects curiosity around his controversial profile amid gangland notoriety. Recent catalysts include a mid-October poll widening Ennis's margin and his active door-to-door campaigning, while low turnout risks and Friday's vote could introduce volatility to these positioning dynamics.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Dublin Central by-election market strongly backs independent Daniel Ennis at 75.5% implied probability, propelled by local polls showing his double-digit lead over rivals, fueled by voter frustration with established parties on housing and crime issues. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan trails at 13.1%, supported by organizational strength but hampered by national headwinds. Gerry Hutch's 3.6% niche reflects curiosity around his controversial profile amid gangland notoriety. Recent catalysts include a mid-October poll widening Ennis's margin and his active door-to-door campaigning, while low turnout risks and Friday's vote could introduce volatility to these positioning dynamics.

question: US-Iran nuclear deal by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if an official agreement over Iranian nuclear research and/or nuclear weapon development, defined as a publicly announced mutual agreement, is reached between the United States and Iran by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If such an agreement is officially reached before the resolution date, this market will resolve to "Yes", regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

Agreements that include the United States and Iran as parties, even if they also involve other countries (e.g., a multilateral deal like the JCPOA), will qualify for resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the United States and/or the Islamic Republic of Iran, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

events.title: US-Iran nuclear deal by March 31?
events.description:

Agreements that include the United States and Iran as parties, even if they also involve other countries (e.g., a multilateral deal like the JCPOA), will qualify for resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the United States and/or the Islamic Republic of Iran, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming an agreement has been reached will also qualify.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 98.3% for a US-Iran nuclear deal by March 31, reflecting stalled indirect Oman-mediated talks and irreconcilable demands over Iran's near-weapons-grade uranium enrichment, IAEA monitoring access, and full US sanctions relief including IRGC delisting. Recent developments, including failed early March negotiations and IAEA reports on Iran's non-compliance amid proxy attacks on US and Israeli interests, underscore diplomatic gridlock. US election-year priorities further diminish prospects, with no scheduled talks before the deadline. High confidence stems from years of post-JCPOA revival failures; only a surprise concession by either side could alter odds, though traders view this as improbable.

question: People's Bank of China rate cut by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the People’s Bank of China (PBoC) cuts the 7-day reverse repo rate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM China Standard Time (CST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A “cut” refers to any lowering of the 7-day reverse repo rate to a level lower than the most recent effective 7-day reverse repo rate.

An official announcement of a cut to the PBoC 7-day Reverse Repo Rate within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of when the rate cut is stated to go into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the People’s Bank of China, including PBoC Open Market Operations announcements (https://www.pbc.gov.cn/en/3688110/3688181/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting on a cut to the 7-day reverse repo rate may also be used.

events.title: People's Bank of China rate cut by March 31?

A “cut” refers to any lowering of the 7-day reverse repo rate to a level lower than the most recent effective 7-day reverse repo rate.

An official announcement of a cut to the PBoC 7-day Reverse Repo Rate within this market’s timeframe will be sufficient for a “Yes” resolution, regardless of when the rate cut is stated to go into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the People’s Bank of China, including PBoC Open Market Operations announcements (https://www.pbc.gov.cn/en/3688110/3688181/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting on a cut to the 7-day reverse repo rate may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders' overwhelming 99.5% consensus against a People's Bank of China (PBOC) rate cut by March 31 reflects the central bank's hawkish stance on key policy rates amid deflationary pressures managed through alternative tools. Recent developments, including the February RRR cut and steady March LPR at 3.45% for one-year loans, underscore PBOC's preference for reserve adjustments over outright rate reductions to avoid moral hazard in lending. Official communications emphasize structural stimulus over broad easing, with market-implied odds bolstered by stable MLF operations at 2.5%. Tail risks include a sharper GDP contraction or yuan depreciation forcing an emergency cut, though consensus forecasts project policy continuity into Q2.

question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on no charges against Les Wexner by June 30 stems from the complete absence of any federal indictments, DOJ announcements, or state-level probes into his Epstein ties. Unsealed court documents from January 2024 named Wexner as a longtime associate but offered no evidence of criminal facilitation or abuse, emphasizing his 2007 break with Epstein and victim status in some financial claims. No grand jury leaks, FBI actions, or prosecutor statements have emerged since, with statutes of limitations likely expired for pre-2010 allegations. This evidentiary vacuum, absent fresh catalysts before the deadline, underpins the 88.5% implied No probability.

question: Les Wexner arrested by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Leslie Wexner is arrested or detained by law enforcement by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Temporary holding at a detention facility while awaiting a judge’s decision on whether to grant a detention warrant qualifies, so long as they are held by law enforcement in an official capacity during that time.

A qualifying arrest/detention includes:
* Being taken into physical custody by law enforcement authorities (including federal, state, local, military, or international law enforcement)
* Voluntarily surrendering to law enforcement authorities in response to an arrest warrant
* Being formally booked or processed following detention
* Being handcuffed by a state authority and taken to a police station
* Surrendering at a police station or courthouse with their attorney
* Being placed under house arrest or electronic monitoring

The following scenarios will NOT qualify as an arrest/detention
* An arrest warrant being issued but not executed
* Being questioned or interviewed without arrest
* Being named in an indictment without arrest

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the arresting government and law enforcement; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

At 87, the retail mogul behind Victoria's Secret's cultural empire faces no active charges from DOJ or state authorities, with recent Epstein document releases yielding zero new accusations against him. question: Will Congress pass any tariffs by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if both the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate pass the same bill, measure, or resolution that explicitly seeks to create a tariff on any country or set of countries for any goods by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

A bill, measure or resolution will be considered to “seek to create a tariff” if it explicitly calls for or orders the imposition of any import tax or duty on any category of goods from any country or region. Category-specific tariffs, general tariffs on countries, or blanket global tariffs will all qualify. The delegation of tariff powers to other government authorities (e.g. the President) without calling for specific tariffs to be imposed, trade restrictions which do not impose an import tax or duty, or tariffs imposed under existing executive authority without new passage by both chambers of Congress will not qualify.

A measure amended by either chamber will only qualify if the amended version is subsequently passed by both chambers in identical form.

The resolution source will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

The resolution source will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 99.3% implied probability of "No" for Congress passing any tariffs by March 31, driven primarily by the executive branch's broad authority under Section 232 and 301 to impose tariffs without legislative approval, as evidenced by President-elect Trump's recent announcements targeting Mexico, Canada, and China via executive orders. No tariff bills have advanced in the lame-duck or incoming 119th Congress, amid priorities like government funding deadlines and debt ceiling talks that crowd out fast-tracked votes. The legislative process—introduction, committee markup, and bicameral passage—typically spans months, reinforcing high confidence. Tail risks include a sudden trade crisis or bipartisan border-security linkage prompting an emergency bill, though such scenarios remain improbable given partisan divides.

question: Will the U.S. tariff rate on China be less than 5% on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve according to the general tariff rate on imports into the United States from the People's Republic of China on March 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

The general tariff rate refers to the base tariff rate paid on imports, including any general tariff the U.S. imposes on all imports (e.g. a 10% tariff on all U.S. imports and a 10% tariff on top of that on Chinese imports would equal a 20% tariff).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Item specific exceptions or increases will not be considered (i.e. this market does not refer to the effective tariff rate).

Only tariffs which are in effect will qualify. Tariffs which are paused, or which have been announced but have not yet gone into effect will not be considered.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible information will also be used.

question: UEFA Europa League: Unbeaten Champion
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the 2025-26 UEFA Europa League champion goes unbeaten during the Knockout Stages of the 2025-26 UEFA Europa League competition. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, “unbeaten” is defined as having not recorded a loss during any match in any stage of the Knockout Stages. The “Knockout Stages” include every match starting with the Knockout Play-Offs carrying each round of matches through, and including, the Europa League Final.

If the 2025-26 Europa League competition is cancelled, postponed after June 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, and a team is confirmed as unbeaten through the completed matches prior to cancellation or postponement, this market will resolve to “Yes”. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from UEFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Will a 10% US blanket tariff be in effect on March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a general or blanket US tariff of 10% or more on imports into the United States is in effect on March 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A general or blanket tariff is a tariff policy that applies a baseline tariff rate of 10% or greater to imports broadly, rather than being limited to a narrow set of products or countries. A tariff that includes item-specific, country-specific, or other limited exceptions will still qualify, as long as a baseline policy of a 10% or greater tariff on imports into the United States is in effect at the specified time.

“In effect" means the tariff must be operational in its application to US imports at the specified time. General or blanket tariffs which go into effect, but are then blocked, injuncted, repealed, or otherwise invalidated such that a blanket tariff rate of 10% or more is not being imposed on imports into the United States at this market’s resolution time will not count.

This market's primary resolution source will be official information from the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible information may also be used.

question: Spread: CA Unión (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CA Unión" if CA Unión win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CA Talleres".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaprofesional.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

In the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CA Talleres" if CA Talleres win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "CA Unión".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligaprofesional.ar. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Unión vs. CA Talleres - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 8:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment leans toward Talleres de Córdoba as favorites against Unión de Santa Fe in their Argentine Primera División matchup, driven by Talleres' stronger recent form with three wins in their last five league games and a robust away record. Unión has faltered defensively, shipping goals in four straight home fixtures amid injury concerns for key midfielder Mauro Luna Diale. Official lineups confirm Talleres' attacking duo of Sosa and Barticciotto available, boosting their edge in head-to-heads where they've won two of the last three. Upcoming factors include Unión's home crowd at Estadio 15 de Abril and Talleres' potential fatigue from Copa Libertadores commitments, tempering the implied probabilities.

events.title: CA Rosario Central vs. CA Tigre - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for March 6 at 8:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rosario Central's robust home form at Estadio Gigante de Arroyito drives trader consensus favoring them over CA Tigre in this Liga Profesional clash, with odds implying a clear edge amid Central's unbeaten streak in their last five league home games. Recent developments include midfielder Jaminton Campaz's return from suspension, bolstering Central's attack after their 2-1 win over Godoy Cruz, while Tigre labors through a three-match winless away skid and defensive injuries to key center-back Abel Luciatti. Head-to-head history favors Central with victories in the prior two meetings, though Tigre's counterpunch under coach Diego Martínez poses upset risk, especially with midweek Copa Argentina fatigue potentially tilting the scales in the high-stakes table battle.

question: Will the Chicago Bears leave Illinois by Dec. 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Chicago Bears franchise of the NFL officially announce a plan to move and play their home games permanently anywhere outside of the state of Illinois by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, any temporary change in venue will not be considered.

For the purpose of this market, no naming changes will be taken into consideration, only official announcements about the home playing field of the Chicago Bears franchise.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Chicago Bears franchise and/or the NFL.

question: Lugano: Dominic Stephan Stricker vs Hugo Grenier
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Dominic Stephan Stricker and Hugo Grenier in the Lugano, scheduled for February 23 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Stricker' if Dominic Stephan Stricker advances against Hugo Grenier.

This market will resolve to 'Grenier' if Hugo Grenier advances against Dominic Stephan Stricker.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Katy Perry confirmed pregnant by June 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Katy perry announces that she is pregnant between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Recent 2-1 wins over Charlotte FC and Columbus Crew highlight their attacking momentum led by Luciano Acosta's eight goals and 10 assists, while CF Montréal languishes near the Eastern Conference basement, winless in seven away games and conceding 2.4 goals per road outing amid key absences like defender Victor Loturi (suspended) and forward Romell Quioto (injured). Head-to-head, Cincinnati has won the last four meetings, including a 5-1 thrashing last August, though Montréal's counterattacking threat under new coach Laurent Courtois introduces upset potential if Cincinnati's defense rests starters ahead of midweek CONCACAF Champions Cup duty.

events.title: CA Sarmiento vs. CA Aldosivi
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Primera División Argentina game, scheduled for Sunday, March 22, 2026 between CA Sarmiento and CA Aldosivi.

eventMetadata.context_description: CA Sarmiento's home advantage at Estadio Eva Perón and superior recent form anchor their 42% trader consensus lead over CA Aldosivi in this Primera Nacional clash. Sarmiento has secured three wins in their last five home outings, with a stout defense conceding just two goals, while Aldosivi languishes on a four-match road winless skid amid relegation pressures. Official injury reports confirm no absences for Sarmiento's key attackers, though Aldosivi's midfielder suspension tilts possession odds further. Head-to-head records favor Sarmiento unbeaten in three prior meetings, supporting the draw's 33% pricing as a hedge against Aldosivi's occasional counter resilience. Momentum and venue edge shape these probabilities amid tight Group B standings.

events.title: Cruzeiro EC vs. Santos FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Brazil Série A game, scheduled for Sunday, March 22, 2026 between Cruzeiro EC and Santos FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cruzeiro's robust home form at Mineirão Stadium, where they've won four of their last five matches, anchors trader consensus at 50.5% implied probability for victory over Santos FC. Santos' struggles on the road—losing three straight away fixtures—combined with a midweek fatigue from Copa Libertadores qualifiers, explain their trailing 22.5% odds, while the draw at 28% reflects tight historical head-to-heads (three draws in the last five meetings). Recent developments bolstering Cruzeiro include full fitness for star forward Arthur Gomes after a minor knock, per official injury reports, versus Santos' confirmed absence of midfielder João Schmidt due to suspension, shifting momentum toward the hosts ahead of this Serie A clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vasco da Gama's home advantage at São Januário anchors trader consensus at 44.5% implied probability for a win, bolstered by their solid recent form with two victories in the last three Brasileirão matches and a strong record against Grêmio (unbeaten in the last four head-to-heads).
